title: Stainless steel cleaning paste
subtitle: A bicarbonate and castile soap paste for sinks, hobs, and appliances
excerpt: Bicarbonate of soda is a mild alkali and a gentle abrasive. Mixed with castile liquid soap and lemon essential oil, it lifts grease and watermarks from stainless steel surfaces without scratching. Apply with a damp cloth, rub with the grain, and rinse well.
sourceNotes: Bicarbonate cleaning chemistry from The Complete Book of Clean, Lim (2019). Castile soap and bicarbonate formulation from Wellness Mama (wellnessmama.com, May 2026). Lemon EO properties from supplier notes.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 small jar (~200 g) of cleaning paste
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. The paste consistency depends on the castile soap brand: add soap gradually until a thick paste forms.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in advance and store in a jar with a tight lid. The paste may dry out slightly on the surface: give it a quick stir before use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months in a sealed jar. The castile soap base is the limiting factor. The paste may separate or dry on the surface: stir to recombine. Discard if it develops an off smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A concentrated liquid soap made from plant oils: traditionally 100% olive oil, though modern versions often blend olive and coconut. Dilute before use in cleaning products. Reacts with acids (citric acid, vinegar) to form a cloudy, separated mixture: do not combine with acids in this formula.

content.text: Stainless steel has a directional grain visible as fine parallel lines. Always rub cleaning pastes with the grain, not against it: cross-grain rubbing leaves visible scratches. Rinse thoroughly after using bicarbonate-based cleaners, as any residue left to dry can leave a dull white film. This paste should not be used on chrome fittings, polished aluminium, or non-stick surfaces.
Do not add vinegar or citric acid to this formula:
reacts with acids and the mixture will separate into a cloudy, curdled liquid. Keep acid-based cleaners separate.

items.prepNote: The mild abrasive and alkali base
Concentrated; adds surfactant action; add gradually to reach paste consistency
Approximately 1 ml; cutting grease and fragrance

content.text: Place the bicarbonate of soda in a bowl. Add the lemon essential oil and stir through. Add the castile liquid soap gradually, stirring after each addition, until the mixture forms a thick paste similar in consistency to toothpaste. Transfer to a jar with a tight-fitting lid.

To use: apply a small amount of paste to a damp cloth. Rub the stainless steel surface with the grain using light, even pressure. Rinse thoroughly with clean water and dry with a soft cloth to prevent watermarks.
3 months in a sealed jar. If the surface of the paste dries out, stir in a few drops of water to restore the texture. Discard if it develops an off smell.
